title: ricinocarpus benguelensis
intro: Ricinocarpus benguelensis, also known as the Benguela tree, is a plant species in the family Sapindaceae, native to Angola. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is rich in flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds that contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Ricinocarpus benguelensis can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world.
